{"step_by_step_thinking": "Rickets is known to cause delayed dentition due to vitamin D and calcium deficiencies affecting bone and tooth development. Congenital hyperthyroidism can also lead to delayed eruption as thyroid hormones play a role in bone and tooth development. Cleidocranial dysplasia is a condition that affects bone development, including delayed eruption of teeth. Cherubism, on the other hand, is a disorder that causes enlargement of the jaws and displacement of the teeth, but it does not typically cause delayed eruption of teeth.", "answer_choice": "D"}
